summary:
The test passed. The output now includes "OK: conflict notified", which means the bug is fixed. The `test.sh` script now exits with a "PASS" message. The core of the problem was that the `informerUpdate` function would silently delete an "assumed" entry if a newer version of an object was received. The fix was to add a notification mechanism, which in this simplified test case was flipping a boolean flag. In a real-world scenario, this would likely involve a callback or a channel communication.

I have successfully found the needle and fixed the bug.
